extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: Starts high (above 0.6) and drops steeply to a low below 0.2 within the first 25% of the time.
R2: Reaches its global minimum (around 0.1–0.2) roughly in the first third of the series.
R3: Then rises to a mid–high plateau (around 0.4–0.55) and stays relatively stable without strong downward trend.
R4: Ends at a value that is equal to or higher than the mid–segment plateau (no clear decline in the last quarter).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: Starts high (above 0.6) but shows a pronounced secondary local peak very early (within the first 10–15% of time).
R2: After the initial steep drop, the values remain more irregular with visible oscillations rather than a smooth descent.
R3: The main rising segment from the global minimum leads to a plateau that is reached earlier (around the middle of the series) and tends to be slightly lower or flatter than in Class 0.
R4: The final part of the series shows either a slight decline or stays at about the same level as the plateau, without the late upward push typical for Class 0.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: Starts high (above ~0.6) and drops steeply to below ~0.25 within the first ~15 time steps.  
R2: Reaches its global minimum (around 0.1–0.15) later, between about time 25 and 45.  
R3: After that minimum, rises to a mid‑level plateau of roughly 0.4–0.55.  
R4: In the last quarter of the series, either stays roughly flat in that mid‑range or rises only moderately.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: Starts high (above ~0.6) with an early sharp spike/peak above ~0.8 within the first ~5 time steps.  
R2: Drops steeply but only reaches its global minimum (around 0.1–0.15) by roughly time 30–40.  
R3: After that minimum, rises more strongly to about 0.45–0.6 and then remains around that level.  
R4: In the last quarter of the series, does not show a renewed upward trend; it stays flat or gently declines from that mid‑high level.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts high (above ~0.6) and quickly drops below ~0.3 within the first ~20 time steps.  
R2: After this drop, the series forms a broad U-shape bottoming near ~0.1–0.15 roughly between time steps 20–40.  
R3: From this minimum, the series rises and then stays in the mid range (~0.35–0.55) without returning to high values (>~0.6) for the remainder of the series.  
R4: The final segment (last ~20 time steps) is flat or gently rising, not clearly trending down.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts high (above ~0.6) and shows a sharp spike/peak above ~0.8 very early (first ~5 time steps) before dropping.  
R2: It then declines to a low region around ~0.1–0.2 between about time steps 20–35, similar depth to Class 0.  
R3: From this low, it rises to a mid range (~0.35–0.55) but then either slightly decreases or stays nearly flat, without a clear upward trend in the final segment.  
R4: Compared with Class 0, the last ~20 time steps are flat or slightly downward, lacking a sustained late increase.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: Within the first 10–15% of the series, the value drops steeply from a high level (~0.7–0.8) to a much lower level (< 0.3).  
R2: Around the middle of the series (roughly 30–40% in), the signal reaches a clear global minimum near 0.1.  
R3: After this minimum, the series rises smoothly and monotonically for a long segment (from about 30–35% to about 60–70% of the series).  
R4: In the last 20–30% of the series, the signal is relatively flat or only gently varying, without a strong final downward trend.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: Within the first 10–15% of the series, the value first rises to a sharp local peak (> 0.8) and then drops steeply.  
R2: Around the middle of the series, the signal again reaches a low near 0.1, but this minimum is followed quickly by a pronounced rise.  
R3: From about 35–40% to about 60–70% of the series, the signal climbs more abruptly than in Class 0, forming a clear mid-series “hill” (peak around 0.5).  
R4: In the last 20–30% of the series, the signal tends to drift slightly downward or stay around a moderate level (~0.4–0.5), without the late strong upward trend sometimes seen in Class 0.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the very beginning (first ~5% of the series), the value starts high (≥ 0.6) and then decreases steeply.  
R2: In the early part (up to about 35–40% of the series), the signal continues to fall and reaches a clear minimum below 0.15.  
R3: After this minimum, the series rises and stays mostly in a mid-range band between 0.35 and 0.55 for a long central portion.  
R4: In the last 20–25% of the series, the values either stay roughly in that same mid-range band or rise slightly, but do not show a strong, sustained upward ramp from very low values.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: In the very beginning (first ~5% of the series), the value also starts high (≥ 0.6) but includes a sharp, narrow spike (or peak) exceeding about 0.8.  
R2: As in class 0, the early part (up to about 35–40% of the series) then drops and reaches a minimum below 0.15.  
R3: After the minimum, the series rises more steeply from low values (≈0.1–0.2) to the mid-range, forming a pronounced upward ramp.  
R4: In the central-to-late portion, the signal tends to peak slightly earlier and then gently decline or flatten, rather than remaining flat and then rising again near the end.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the initial sharp drop (around time 0–10), the series reaches a clear minimum below 0.15 roughly between time 20 and 40.  
R2: From that minimum, the series then rises steadily so that by time 50–60 it is above 0.4.  
R3: In the last third of the series (after about time 60), values stay roughly in the mid‑range (about 0.35–0.55) with no strong downward trend.  
R4: The final value is not lower than 0.3 (it is around or above the mid‑range reached after the rise).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the initial sharp drop (around time 0–10), the series still shows a pronounced dip below 0.15 between time 20 and 40, similar to Class 0.  
R2: From that minimum, the rise is steeper and earlier so that by time 40–50 the series is already around or above 0.45.  
R3: In the middle to late part (about time 50–80), values remain mostly near the higher mid‑range (about 0.45–0.55) without a secondary rise toward the very end.  
R4: The final segment (last ~15 time units) is flat or slightly decreasing around 0.35–0.45, not clearly trending upward.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the initial drop, the series reaches its global minimum around time 30–40 and then rises steadily.
R2: In the interval time 40–80, the series stays mostly between 0.4 and 0.55 with only small oscillations (no sharp peaks).
R3: Near the end (after time ~80), the series either stays flat or shows only a modest increase (no pronounced final peak above ~0.6).
R4: The highest value of the whole series is at the very beginning and is not exceeded later.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the initial drop, the series reaches its global minimum around time 30–40 and then rises steadily.
R2: In the interval time 40–70, the series has a noticeable single broad peak around 0.5–0.55, followed by a decline.
R3: After this mid‑series peak, the values tend to decrease or flatten and stay mostly below ~0.5 toward the end (no strong late rise).
R4: The early segment (first 20 time steps) shows stronger short‑scale oscillations during the initial drop than in Class 0 (more jagged descent).

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts high (above ≈0.6) and drops steeply to a minimum below ≈0.15 within the first ≈30 time steps.  
R2: After this minimum, the series rises to a mid-level plateau between ≈0.4 and ≈0.55 around time steps 30–70.  
R3: In the last ≈20 time steps, the series either stays flat or rises slightly, ending below or around ≈0.55 (does not exceed its early maximum).  
R4: The global maximum occurs near the beginning of the series, not near the end.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts high (above ≈0.6) and drops steeply to a minimum below ≈0.15 within the first ≈30 time steps.  
R2: After this minimum, the series rises to a mid-level plateau between ≈0.4 and ≈0.55 around time steps 30–70.  
R3: In the last ≈20 time steps, the series is flat or slightly decreasing, ending below ≈0.5 and not higher than the plateau just before it.  
R4: The global maximum occurs within the first ≈10 time steps, and the last ≈20 time steps are strictly lower than the maximum reached around time steps 40–60.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the very early part (first ~10% of time), the series starts high (around 0.6–0.8) and then drops steeply.  
R2: After this drop, the series reaches a clear minimum in the first third of the sequence (value around 0.1–0.2).  
R3: From this minimum up to about two‑thirds of the sequence, the series rises and then stays roughly in the mid‑range (around 0.4–0.6) without any strong downward trend.  
R4: In the last ~20% of time, the series is flat or slightly rising, and does not show a clear overall decline.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: In the very early part (first ~10% of time), the series also starts high (around 0.6–0.9) and drops steeply.  
R2: After this drop, the series reaches a clear minimum in the first third (around 0.1–0.2), similar to Class 0.  
R3: From this minimum up to roughly the midpoint, the series rises into the mid‑range (around 0.4–0.6) and then begins a gentle but persistent downward trend.  
R4: In the last ~20% of time, the series is overall slightly decreasing or lower than its earlier mid‑segment level (around 0.3–0.4), rather than flat or increasing.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the initial sharp drop (around time 5–10), the series reaches a clear global minimum roughly in the middle of the window (around time 30–40).  
R2: This middle minimum is quite low (around 0.1–0.15).  
R3: After this middle minimum, the series rises steadily and ends clearly higher than that minimum (end value > 0.35).  
R4: The rise after the middle minimum is relatively smooth, without large oscillations or a pronounced second deep dip.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the initial sharp drop (around time 5–10), the series keeps decreasing and its lowest point is reached relatively early, before the middle of the window (before about time 30).  
R2: After that early minimum, the series rises and forms a broad “hump” (plateau or gentle peak) roughly in the middle of the window (around time 35–60).  
R3: This middle hump stays in a moderate range (about 0.4–0.55), not rising as high as the very beginning peak.  
R4: Towards the end, the series is roughly flat or slightly declining from this middle hump, without a strong late upward trend.
